wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GreenAxis project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as an integrated web solution for environmental service companies, combining a high-performance public website with an administrative CMS. Document the key features including mobile-first responsive design, rich content management capabilities, media library system, and security measures. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the platform's capabilities and target use cases for environmental service businesses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Next.js App Router Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Next.js App Router architecture in GreenAxis. Explain the page-based routing system, dynamic routes with slug parameters, and file-based routing conventions. Document the root layout structure, metadata generation from database, and middleware implementation for security headers. Detail server components vs client components distinction, route groups for organization, and error handling patterns. Include examples of nested layouts, loading states, and parallel routes. Address performance optimizations like automatic code splitting and ISR/SSG considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Routes Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API routes documentation for GreenAxis Next.js implementation. Document the App Router API pattern, request/response handling, and server action integration. Explain endpoint organization by feature areas (admin, auth, content management). Detail HTTP method usage, URL routing patterns, and parameter handling. Include request validation, error handling strategies, and response formatting standards. Document authentication requirements for protected endpoints and CORS configuration. Provide examples of CRUD operations, file uploads, and media management through API endpoints. Address performance optimization techniques and rate limiting considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Model Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model overview documentation for the GreenAxis application. Explain the overall database architecture, Prisma ORM configuration with LibSQL adapter, and Turso distributed database integration. Document the centralized PlatformConfig model as the main configuration hub and how it relates to all other entities. Detail the database provider setup, connection management, and migration strategies. Include Prisma client initialization, transaction handling, and error management patterns. Explain the relationship between SQLite provider and LibSQL adapter for distributed database capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Public Website Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the public website features of GreenAxis. Document the landing page implementation with hero carousel functionality, services catalog system including service listing and individual service detail pages, news/blog system with pagination and article filtering, about us page content management, contact form functionality with validation and submission handling, and legal pages management for terms and privacy. Explain the component architecture, routing patterns, data fetching strategies, and user experience optimizations. Include implementation details for responsive design, SEO optimization, and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document all authentication-related HTTP endpoints including login (/api/auth/login), logout (/api/auth/logout), account setup (/api/auth/setup), password reset (/api/auth/reset-password), account deletion (/api/auth/delete-account), and session check (/api/auth/check). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (POST/GET), request/response schemas, authentication requirements, session token handling, and security considerations. Include detailed parameter validation rules, error response formats, and status codes. Document the authentication flow, session management, CSRF protection, and rate limiting policies. Provide client implementation examples, common integration patterns, and troubleshooting guidance for authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for GreenAxis. Detail the bcrypt password hashing implementation with 12 rounds of encryption, session-based authentication with secure cookie management, and user registration flow. Document the authentication middleware, login/logout endpoints, password reset functionality with unique tokens, and account setup process. Explain session token generation, expiration handling, and secure cookie attributes. Include implementation details for user validation, credential verification, and authentication state management. Address security measures against brute force attacks, session hijacking prevention, and secure password handling practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the GreenAxis admin dashboard system. Document the dashboard layout structure, navigation menu organization, and administrative section cards with icons and descriptions. Explain the grid-based section layout, responsive design implementation, and external link handling for additional services. Include details on the admin navigation pattern, section categorization, and user experience flow from dashboard to specific administrative areas.

wiki_catalogs.name: Main Editor Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main EditorJS component implementation. Document the EditorJSComponent wrapper that initializes the Editor.js instance, handles dynamic imports for tools, manages editor lifecycle, and provides internationalization support. Explain the component's props interface, state management, lazy loading patterns, and integration with React's useEffect hooks. Cover the initialization process, tool registration, configuration options, and event handling. Include examples of editor instantiation, data persistence, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Landing Page & Hero Carousel
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the landing page and hero carousel implementation. Document the main page layout structure, hero carousel component functionality including image display, navigation controls, auto-play features, and responsive behavior. Explain the carousel data management system, API integration for carousel content, and dynamic image loading from Cloudinary. Include implementation details for component state management, user interactions, accessibility features, and performance optimizations. Provide examples of carousel configuration, content updates, and troubleshooting common issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloudinary Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Cloudinary integration including URL optimization utilities, transformation parameter injection, and preset helpers for different use cases. Document the isCloudinaryUrl function for URL validation, getCloudinaryImageUrl function for applying format, quality, and width transformations, and various preset functions like getHeroImageUrl, getThumbnailImageUrl, and getAdminThumbnailUrl. Explain the transformation string construction, URL parsing logic, and how existing transformations are handled. Include examples of optimization presets for hero images, thumbnails, service images, and admin interfaces. Document the responsive URL helpers for Next.js Image component integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Tool Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Editor.js Image Tool implementation. Detail the custom image tool architecture, upload handler integration, and media picker functionality. Document the tool configuration options, file validation rules, supported image formats, and size limitations. Explain the integration with Cloudinary for image optimization and the media library browser for existing asset selection. Include implementation details for drag-and-drop functionality, alt text handling, and responsive image display. Provide code examples showing tool registration, configuration patterns, and custom event handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Services Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the services management interface in GreenAxis. Detail the complete CRUD operations for services including creation, editing, deletion, and activation/deactivation. Document the rich text editor integration with Editor.js for detailed content management, including block-based content structure and serialization. Explain the icon selection system with categorized icons (nature, water, air, management, infrastructure), drag-and-drop ordering functionality, and media upload capabilities. Cover the featured services toggle, service status management, and the responsive card-based interface design. Include implementation details for slug generation, content validation, and the integration with the backend API endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the GreenAxis project. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js, Bun runtime, and database setup requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions from repository cloning through dependency installation. Document environment variable configuration for Turso database, Cloudinary, and Resend services. Include initial project setup, database migration execution, and local development server startup. Cover basic project structure navigation and first-time configuration steps. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for new developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System & UI Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the component system architecture. Document the shadcn/ui component library integration, custom component development patterns, and component composition strategies. Explain the layout component hierarchy including public-layout.tsx and admin-layout.tsx, their props, and usage patterns. Detail the component folder structure, naming conventions, and reusability patterns. Include examples of form components, data display components, and interactive elements. Document component props validation, TypeScript integration, and accessibility features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed authentication system documentation for GreenAxis. Document the bcrypt-based password hashing implementation, session management with secure cookies, and authentication middleware integration. Explain login/logout workflows, token generation and validation, and user session lifecycle management. Detail password reset functionality, account setup procedures, and security measures including CSRF protection and rate limiting. Include authentication state management, protected route enforcement, and user permission handling. Document integration with NextAuth patterns and cookie security configurations. Address common authentication scenarios, error handling, and troubleshooting authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core database entities in GreenAxis. Document the PlatformConfig model as the central configuration hub covering site settings, contact information, social media links, SEO configuration, and theme customization. Detail the Service model for the services catalog including slug generation, ordering, and featured content management. Explain the News model for blog functionality with markdown content, Editor.js blocks, publishing workflow, and featured content. Document the SiteImage model for media management with duplicate detection via SHA-256 hashing. Cover the CarouselSlide model for hero carousel functionality with customization options. Include field definitions, validation rules, default values, and business logic constraints for each entity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the administrative features of GreenAxis. Document the admin dashboard design and layout structure, content management system for services and news editing, media library browser implementation with upload and organization capabilities, user management and permission systems, configuration settings interface, and contact message management. Explain the administrative workflow, security considerations, and integration patterns between admin components. Include implementation details for drag-and-drop reordering, bulk operations, and content validation mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for administrative endpoints used by the CMS system. Document configuration management (/api/admin/config), media library operations (/api/admin/media/*), image management (/api/admin/images), and reference checking (/api/admin/media/check-references). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and authorization levels. Include media upload handling, duplicate detection mechanisms, reference tracking, external media integration, and bulk operations. Document parameter validation, error handling, file size limits, supported formats, and security restrictions. Provide examples of administrative workflows, batch operations, and integration patterns for content management tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed authorization and permissions documentation for GreenAxis. Explain role-based access control implementation, middleware-based route protection, and permission checking mechanisms. Document the admin panel access controls, protected route enforcement, and user role management. Detail the authorization middleware configuration, route-level security restrictions, and session-based permission validation. Include examples of protected endpoints, admin-only features, and user-specific content access. Address permission inheritance, role hierarchy, and security boundary enforcement throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the content management system within GreenAxis. Document the services management interface including CRUD operations, drag-and-drop reordering, and content validation. Explain the news/blog management system with rich text editing capabilities, publishing workflows, and content organization. Cover the 'About Us' page management including section editing and content updates. Include implementation details for content persistence, validation rules, and user interface patterns used across all content management areas.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Tools Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for all custom Editor.js tools implementation. Document each specialized tool including image, video, audio, header, link, color, marker, and strikethrough tools. Explain the tool architecture, configuration patterns, upload handlers, and integration with the media library system. Cover the custom tool development process, plugin registration, and tool-specific features like file validation, size limits, and media picker integration. Include implementation details for each tool's functionality, configuration options, and usage patterns within the content management workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Services Catalog System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the services catalog system. Document the services listing page implementation, individual service detail pages with slug-based routing, service data models, and content management integration. Explain the services section component architecture, dynamic content rendering, and SEO optimization for service pages. Include details about service categorization, filtering capabilities, image handling through Cloudinary, and responsive design patterns. Document the API endpoints for services data, content editing workflows, and performance considerations for service listings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Library Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the media library browser component including media listing, filtering, search functionality, and media card display system. Document the MediaLibraryBrowser component implementation, MediaCard component for individual media items, and MediaPreviewModal for detailed viewing. Explain media loading strategies, pagination handling, search and filter mechanisms, drag-and-drop reordering, and bulk selection capabilities. Include implementation details for media metadata display, status indicators, and interaction patterns. Document the integration with Cloudinary for media optimization and the preview modal functionality for detailed media inspection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Tool Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Editor.js Video Tool implementation. Document the video upload handler, file validation for video formats, and integration with the media library system. Explain the tool's configuration options, supported video codecs, size limits, and quality settings. Cover the video player integration, thumbnail generation, and playback controls. Include implementation details for video metadata handling, duration calculation, and responsive video display. Provide examples of tool registration, custom configuration, and integration with the content management workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: News/Blog Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the news/blog management system within the administrative interface. Document the complete content lifecycle from creation to publication, including draft management, scheduling, and content organization. Explain the rich text editing capabilities with Editor.js integration, media embedding, and content formatting options. Cover the news listing interface with filtering, search functionality, and bulk operations. Detail the slug generation system for URL-friendly permalinks, content preview capabilities, and the relationship between news articles and the public-facing news page. Include implementation specifics for content validation, image handling, and the API endpoints used for data persistence.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the GreenAxis technology stack and system architecture. Describe the high-level design using Next.js 16 with App Router, React 19, and TypeScript 5. Document component interactions between frontend (shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS), backend (Next.js API routes, Prisma ORM), and external services (Turso, Cloudinary, Resend). Explain technical decisions including distributed database architecture, media optimization pipeline, and security middleware implementation. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing component relationships and data flows. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling & Theming System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the styling and theming system. Explain Tailwind CSS 4 integration, custom utility classes, and design token usage. Document the theme provider implementation with dark/light mode switching, system preference detection, and persistent theme storage. Detail the color system, typography scale, spacing units, and responsive breakpoints. Include examples of component styling patterns, CSS-in-JS alternatives, and performance optimization techniques. Address accessibility considerations for color contrast and keyboard navigation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security middleware documentation for GreenAxis. Document the implementation of security headers including X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and Strict-Transport-Security. Explain Content-Security-Policy configuration for resource loading restrictions and frame ancestors control. Detail the middleware matcher configuration excluding static assets and Next.js internal routes. Document CORS policy implementation, origin validation, and cross-site request protection. Include rate limiting strategies, request sanitization, and protection against common web vulnerabilities. Address security best practices, header validation, and compliance with security standards. Provide guidance on customizing security policies and monitoring security events.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for content management database entities. Detail the LegalPage model for terms and privacy policy management with markdown fallback and Editor.js block support. Document the AboutPage model as a fully editable content management system covering hero sections, history, mission/vision, values, team, statistics, and certification sections. Explain the ContactMessage model for form submission tracking with consent management and read status. Cover the SocialFeedConfig model for social media integration with platform-specific configurations. Document the PasswordResetToken model for secure password recovery with expiration handling. Include content serialization patterns, JSON field usage, and content validation strategies for each entity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rich Text Editing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the rich text editing system using Editor.js. Document the main editor component implementation, custom tool development including image, video, audio, header, link, color, marker, and strikethrough tools, content serialization and storage mechanisms, and frontend rendering capabilities. Explain the tool architecture, plugin system, content validation, and integration with the media library. Include implementation details for content formatting, undo/redo functionality, and cross-browser compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for content management endpoints. Document service management (/api/servicios), news/blog operations (/api/noticias), about page content (/api/about), carousel management (/api/carrusel), legal pages (/api/legal), and platform configuration (/api/config). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, CRUD operations, request/response schemas, and content validation rules. Include rich text content handling with Editor.js serialization, slug generation, SEO metadata management, and content versioning. Document authentication requirements, authorization levels, and content approval workflows. Provide examples of content creation, updates, deletion, and retrieval operations with proper error handling and validation responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security middleware documentation for GreenAxis. Document the comprehensive security headers implementation including X-Frame-Options (DENY), X-Content-Type-Options (nosniff), X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and Strict-Transport-Security. Explain Content-Security-Policy configuration with frame-ancestors 'none' and resource loading restrictions. Detail the middleware matcher configuration excluding static assets, the security audit compliance with OWASP Top 10 2021 standards, and production-ready security score (8.5/10). Include examples of header configurations, threat mitigation strategies, and security header validation. Address browser compatibility, security header precedence, and CSP policy optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Library Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the media library management system. Document the media browser interface, upload functionality with Cloudinary integration, duplicate detection mechanisms, and reference tracking. Explain the media picker components, modal interfaces, and drag-and-drop upload capabilities. Include details on media organization, filtering, search functionality, and bulk operations. Cover the integration with Cloudinary CDN, file optimization, and media validation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Serialization & Storage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for content serialization and storage mechanisms in the rich text editing system. Document the EditorJS data format, content validation, and storage strategies. Explain the serialization process for different block types, data transformation utilities, and content persistence patterns. Cover the editorDataToText helper function, content extraction methods, and data integrity validation. Include examples of serializing complex content structures, handling nested blocks, and maintaining content consistency across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: News & Blog System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the news and blog system implementation. Document the news listing page with pagination, individual news article detail pages with slug-based routing, and content management workflows. Explain the news section component architecture, rich text content rendering, image optimization through Cloudinary, and SEO best practices for blog posts. Include implementation details for news categorization, search functionality, related articles display, and responsive design patterns. Document the API endpoints for news data, content editing interfaces, and performance optimizations for content-heavy pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Picker Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for media picker components including the main MediaPicker for rich text editing, MediaPickerModal for standalone selection, and MediaPickerCompact for space-constrained interfaces. Document the picker workflow, media selection logic, integration with the media library, and return value handling. Explain the modal-based selection interface, compact inline selection, and how pickers integrate with Editor.js tools. Include implementation details for selection validation, multiple selection support, and picker state management. Document the relationship between pickers and the media library browser.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Tool Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Editor.js Audio Tool implementation. Document the audio file upload handling, format validation for various audio types, and integration with the media library. Explain the tool's configuration including supported audio formats, file size limits, and quality settings. Cover the audio player interface, playback controls, and metadata extraction. Include implementation details for audio file processing, duration display, and responsive audio element rendering. Provide code examples for tool configuration, custom event handling, and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: About Us Page Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the About Us page management system. Detail the dual-interface approach with main About Us content and dedicated About section management. Document the rich text editing capabilities for company information, mission statements, and team introductions. Explain the content organization patterns, image integration for team photos and company imagery, and the relationship between different About page sections. Cover the content validation rules, SEO optimization features, and the integration with the public About Us page display. Include implementation details for content serialization, preview functionality, and the API endpoints supporting both main About content and section-specific content management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design & Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the GreenAxis database schema. Detail entity relationships including PlatformConfig, Service, News, CarouselSlide, SiteImage, Admin, ContactMessage, LegalPage, AboutPage, PasswordResetToken, and SocialFeedConfig. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints for each model. Explain data validation rules and business logic constraints. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document data access patterns using Prisma ORM, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle, retention policies, and archival rules. Include data migration paths and version management. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management & Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the state management architecture and data flow patterns. Explain the use of React hooks for local state, custom hooks like use-toast for notifications, and server actions for data mutations. Detail the client-server data synchronization patterns, error handling strategies, and loading state management. Include examples of form state management, modal state handling, and cross-component communication. Document performance considerations like memoization, debouncing, and efficient re-rendering patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Fetching Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed data fetching patterns documentation for GreenAxis backend. Document server actions implementation, data fetching optimization strategies, and caching mechanisms. Explain Prisma client usage, database query patterns, and transaction handling. Detail media reference checking, duplicate detection algorithms, and file management workflows. Include error handling patterns, data validation strategies, and response formatting standards. Document integration with external services like Cloudinary and Turso database. Address performance optimization techniques, query optimization, and data consistency measures. Provide examples of complex data operations, batch processing, and asynchronous task handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Administration Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for user administration and security-related database entities. Document the Admin model for user account management including email authentication, role-based permissions (developer, superadmin, admin), status tracking (pending, approved, inactive), and password hashing requirements. Detail the PasswordResetToken model for secure password recovery including token generation, uniqueness constraints, expiration handling (1 hour), and usage tracking. Explain the authentication workflow integration with session management and security middleware. Include user role hierarchies, access control patterns, and security best practices embedded in the database design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the media management system. Document Cloudinary integration for media optimization and CDN delivery, media library browser implementation with search and filtering capabilities, media picker components for content insertion, media card display system, preview modal functionality, upload endpoint handling, and reference tracking mechanisms. Explain duplicate detection algorithms, file validation, optimization settings, and storage management. Include implementation details for drag-and-drop upload, batch operations, and media metadata handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Upload API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for media upload and management endpoints. Document file upload operations (/api/upload) and image gallery management (/api/imagenes). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, multipart/form-data handling, file validation rules, size limits, and supported formats. Include Cloudinary integration details, image optimization settings, duplicate detection mechanisms, and reference tracking. Document error handling for invalid files, upload failures, and storage limitations. Provide examples of file upload workflows, batch operations, and integration patterns for media-rich applications. Include security considerations for file uploads, virus scanning, and content filtering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Protection & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive data protection and validation documentation for GreenAxis. Detail database security measures with Prisma ORM, input validation and sanitization practices, and data encryption at rest. Document the Turso database security configuration, query parameter binding, and protection against SQL injection attacks. Explain data validation rules, input sanitization techniques, and secure data transmission practices. Include examples of validated forms, sanitized inputs, and secure database operations. Address data retention policies, GDPR compliance considerations, and secure data deletion procedures. Document error handling without information leakage and secure logging practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the configuration and settings management system for GreenAxis. Explain the platform configuration interface including site name, contact information, social media settings, and branding options. Detail the configuration API endpoints, data validation, and persistence mechanisms. Include implementation details for global settings management, configuration backup and restore, and settings inheritance patterns. Cover the integration with database models and the user interface for configuration updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Rendering System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the frontend rendering system that displays EditorJS content. Document the renderEditorBlocks function, block type handling, and React component generation for different content types. Explain the rendering pipeline for paragraphs, headings, lists, images, quotes, embeds, videos, and audio content. Cover styling integration with Tailwind CSS, responsive design considerations, and accessibility features. Include examples of custom rendering logic, block-level styling, and content presentation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: About Us Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the about us page content management system. Document the complete about page implementation, content editing workflows, and rich text content management through Editor.js. Explain the page structure, content sections organization, media integration capabilities, and responsive design considerations. Include details about content validation, preview functionality, and publishing workflows. Document the API endpoints for about page data, content versioning, and administrative interface for content updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Upload Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for media upload endpoints covering the /api/upload route for direct file uploads and /api/imagenes route for media management. Document HTTP methods, request/response schemas, file validation rules, and upload processing workflows. Explain multipart/form-data handling, file type restrictions, size limits, and Cloudinary integration for media optimization. Include authentication requirements, error handling strategies, and response formats. Document the upload pipeline from file reception through Cloudinary processing to database storage. Address security considerations, validation errors, and upload progress handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text Formatting Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for text formatting tools including headers, color, marker, and strikethrough tools. Document the header tool implementation with level-based formatting, color tool configuration for text highlighting, marker tool for inline annotations, and strikethrough functionality. Explain the tool architecture, configuration patterns, and integration with the Editor.js core. Cover customization options, styling approaches, and keyboard shortcuts. Include implementation details for each tool's specific functionality, event handling, and content serialization. Provide examples of tool registration, custom styling, and advanced formatting workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the frontend architecture of GreenAxis. Describe the Next.js App Router structure with page-based routing and server components. Document component hierarchy including layout components, reusable UI components, and page-specific implementations. Explain styling strategy using Tailwind CSS 4 with shadcn/ui component library integration. Detail state management approaches including React Query for data fetching, Zustand for client-side state, and form handling with React Hook Form. Document responsive design principles with mobile-first approach and accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1). Include component composition patterns, theming system with dark/light mode, and animation implementation using Framer Motion.

wiki_catalogs.name: Responsive Design & Accessibility
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for responsive design implementation and accessibility compliance. Explain the mobile-first approach, breakpoint system, and adaptive component behavior. Document WCAG 2.1 compliance including ARIA labels, semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support. Detail focus management, color contrast ratios, and text scaling accessibility. Include examples of responsive component patterns, touch-friendly interactions, and assistive technology compatibility. Address performance impact of responsive design and optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships & Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database entity relationships and referential constraints in GreenAxis. Explain the one-to-many relationships between PlatformConfig and various content entities, though most entities are independent configuration points. Document the unique constraints for slugs across Service and News models for URL-friendly routing. Detail the ordering mechanisms using integer order fields for carousel slides and content management. Explain the active/published status flags and their impact on content visibility. Document the hash-based duplicate detection system in SiteImage model and its relationship to media management workflows. Include Prisma query patterns, relationship loading strategies, and performance considerations for entity relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact & Public API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for public contact form endpoint. Document the contact submission endpoint (/api/contacto) including HTTP methods, request/response schemas, and form validation rules. Specify required fields, email validation, spam prevention measures, and automated response handling. Include integration examples for frontend contact forms, error handling for validation failures, and success confirmation responses. Document rate limiting policies, IP blocking mechanisms, and security considerations for preventing abuse. Provide client implementation examples and troubleshooting guidance for common contact form integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact Messages Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the contact messages management system. Document the message listing interface, message viewing capabilities, and response handling workflows. Explain the contact form integration, message categorization, and status management. Include details on message persistence, notification systems, and administrative response features. Cover the integration with email services and message archival processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact Form Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the contact form functionality. Document the contact page implementation, form validation mechanisms, user input handling, and submission processing workflows. Explain the form component architecture, validation rules, error handling, and success feedback systems. Include details about form data processing, email notification integration through Resend, spam prevention measures, and data privacy considerations. Document the API endpoint for form submissions, security measures, and troubleshooting common form-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reference Tracking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the media reference tracking system including duplicate detection algorithms, reference counting, and media lifecycle management. Document the mediaReferences utility functions, check-references endpoint implementation, and how media usage is tracked across the application. Explain the relationship between media entities and their references in services, news articles, and other content types. Include implementation details for reference validation, orphaned media cleanup, and duplicate detection mechanisms. Document the API endpoint for checking media references and the utility functions for managing reference relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Link Tool Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Editor.js Link Tool implementation. Document the link creation functionality, URL validation, and target attribute configuration. Explain the tool's integration with internal routing, external link handling, and anchor navigation. Cover the link preview functionality, title customization, and accessibility features. Include implementation details for link validation, sanitization, and secure link handling. Provide examples of tool configuration, custom link validation, and integration with the content management system's URL structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for GreenAxis. Document the Next.js API routes implementation with server actions and data fetching patterns. Explain middleware architecture including security headers, rate limiting, and authentication enforcement. Detail server-side rendering strategies, data fetching optimization, and caching mechanisms. Document the authentication system with bcrypt password hashing, session management, and cookie security. Include API endpoint organization, request/response handling, and error management patterns. Address security middleware implementation, CORS policies, and protection against common web vulnerabilities. Explain integration patterns with external services and data persistence strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Legal Pages Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the legal pages management system. Document the terms and conditions page implementation, privacy policy page structure, and content management workflows for legal documents. Explain the legal page components, content editing through Editor.js, and SEO optimization for legal content. Include details about content validation, version control for legal documents, and administrative access controls. Document the API endpoints for legal page data, content update procedures, and compliance considerations for legal content management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core features implementation of GreenAxis. Document the public website features including landing page with hero carousel, services catalog with detailed pages, news/blog system with pagination, about us page content management, contact form functionality, and legal pages management. Explain the administrative features including dashboard design, content management system, media library implementation, user management and permissions, and configuration settings. Detail the rich text editing capabilities using Editor.js with custom tool development, media management integration with Cloudinary, and drag-and-drop functionality for content reordering. Include implementation patterns, component relationships, and user workflow optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security implementation documentation for GreenAxis. Document the authentication system design with bcrypt password hashing (12 rounds), session management with secure cookies, and rate limiting mechanisms. Explain authorization and permission systems, security middleware configuration with comprehensive headers (CSP, HSTS, XSS protection), and data protection measures. Detail the security audit compliance with OWASP Top 10 2021 standards, production-ready security score (8.5/10), and threat mitigation strategies. Include session token generation, password reset flow with unique tokens, and protection against common attacks including CSRF, XSS, and brute force attempts. Address database security, input validation, and secure data transmission practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all GreenAxis endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each API group. Include detailed endpoint specifications for administrative endpoints (/api/admin/*), authentication routes (/api/auth/*), media management (/api/admin/media/*), content management (/api/servicios, /api/noticias, /api/about), and public endpoints (/api/contacto, /api/upload). Document request/response formats, parameter validation, error handling strategies, and rate limiting policies. Include authentication requirements with session tokens, security considerations for each endpoint, and integration examples. Provide client implementation guidelines, common use cases, and performance optimization tips for API consumers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration documentation for GreenAxis deployment and customization. Document environment variables setup for Turso database connection, Cloudinary media services, Resend email integration, and authentication configuration. Explain database configuration with Turso/LibSQL adapter, Prisma ORM setup, and connection pooling. Detail third-party service integration including Cloudinary CDN configuration, Resend email settings, Google Analytics integration, and social media feed configuration. Include performance optimization settings for Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and database connections. Document deployment configuration, environment-specific settings, and troubleshooting common configuration issues. Address security considerations for production deployments and monitoring setup recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for GreenAxis. Document production deployment strategies including Next.js standalone build configuration, Turso database deployment, and Cloudinary CDN setup. Explain environment configuration for different deployment stages (development, staging, production), monitoring and maintenance procedures, and backup and recovery operations. Detail the data export/import scripts for database migration, automated deployment workflows, and CI/CD pipeline configuration. Include performance monitoring setup, error tracking integration, and operational troubleshooting procedures. Address scaling considerations, load balancing strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Document maintenance schedules, update procedures, and operational best practices for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for GreenAxis. Document unit testing approaches for individual components and utility functions, integration testing methods for API endpoints and database operations, and end-to-end testing implementation for complete user workflows. Explain the existing test suite including media endpoint testing, upload duplicate detection validation, and deletion endpoint verification. Detail quality assurance processes including ESLint configuration, TypeScript compilation checks, and code quality standards. Include testing best practices for Next.js applications, database testing strategies, and API contract testing. Document continuous integration testing, performance testing approaches, and security testing procedures. Address test automation, debugging techniques, and quality metrics collection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for GreenAxis contributors. Document code organization standards including component development patterns, file structure conventions, and naming conventions. Explain the component development lifecycle from initial design through implementation and testing. Detail contributing guidelines including code review processes, pull request procedures, and collaboration workflows. Document code quality standards using ESLint configuration, TypeScript best practices, and architectural consistency requirements. Include development environment setup, debugging techniques, and performance optimization guidelines. Address code documentation standards, commit message conventions, and release management procedures. Provide guidance for extending functionality, adding new features, and maintaining codebase health.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for GreenAxis. Document common issues and their solutions including authentication problems, database connectivity issues, media upload failures, and performance optimization tips. Explain debugging techniques for Next.js applications, database query optimization, and API endpoint troubleshooting. Include frequently asked questions about deployment, configuration, feature usage, and integration challenges. Address performance troubleshooting including database query optimization, image loading issues, and server response times. Document error handling strategies, logging approaches, and monitoring setup for production environments. Provide step-by-step resolution guides for typical development and operational issues, along with preventive measures and best practices.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis of the GreenAxis project. Let me create the blog post.

<blog>

# GreenAxis: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

GreenAxis S.A.S. is an innovative web application designed specifically for environmental services companies. This comprehensive platform combines a high-performance public website with an integrated administrative CMS that enables complete content management without requiring technical programming knowledge.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as a complete digital presence solution for environmental service providers, offering:
- Professional corporate website with modern design
- Integrated content management system (CMS)
- Advanced media management capabilities
- Real-time analytics and SEO optimization
- Secure administration panel

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Deliver a mobile-first responsive experience
- Provide intuitive content management for non-technical users
- Ensure enterprise-grade security and performance
- Offer comprehensive media handling with duplicate detection
- Enable seamless integration with external services

### Target Audience
- Environmental service companies in Colombia
- Corporate clients seeking sustainable solutions
- Organizations requiring professional digital presence
- Businesses needing integrated content management

## Technical Architecture

The GreenAxis project follows a modern full-stack architecture built on Next.js 16 with TypeScript, implementing enterprise-grade security and performance optimizations.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[Server Components]
    C --> D[API Routes]
    D --> E[Prisma ORM]
    E --> F[Turso Database]
    D --> G[Cloudinary CDN]
    D --> H[Resend Email]
    
    I[Admin Panel] --> J[React Components]
    J --> K[Editor.js]
    K --> L[Media Library]
    L --> G
    
    M[Security Middleware] --> B
    M --> D
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer:**
- Next.js 16 with App Router architecture
- React 19 with TypeScript 5
- Tailwind CSS 4 for styling
- shadcn/ui components with Radix UI foundation
- Framer Motion for animations
- Lucide React for consistent iconography

**Backend Layer:**
- Next.js API Routes for server-side operations
- Prisma ORM 6.19.2 with LibSQL adapter
- Turso distributed database
- Server Actions and Server Components
- Zustand for client-side state management
- TanStack React Query for data fetching

**External Services:**
- Cloudinary for media optimization and CDN
- Resend for transactional email delivery
- Google Analytics integration
- Social media embedding capabilities

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key architectural patterns:

**Layered Architecture:** Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers
**Repository Pattern:** Prisma provides data access abstraction
**Factory Pattern:** Dynamic component creation for Editor.js tools
**Observer Pattern:** Theme switching and media library updates
**Command Pattern:** Server Actions for complex operations

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant Next.js
    participant API
    participant Prisma
    participant Database
    
    Client->>+Next.js: Request Page
    Next.js->>+API: Fetch Data
    API->>+Prisma: Query Database
    Prisma->>+Database: Execute Query
    Database-->>-Prisma: Return Results
    Prisma-->>-API: Processed Data
    API-->>-Next.js: JSON Response
    Next.js-->>-Client: Rendered Page
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Root:**
- `src/app/layout.tsx` - Global layout with metadata generation and theme provider
- `src/middleware.ts` - Security middleware with comprehensive headers
- `src/app/page.tsx` - Main landing page implementation

**API Endpoints:**
- `src/app/api/route.ts` - Base API endpoint
- `src/app/api/admin/` - Administrative endpoints
- `src/app/api/auth/` - Authentication routes
- `src/app/api/upload/` - Media upload handling

### Core Modules

**Database Layer:**
- `src/lib/db.ts` - Prisma client configuration with LibSQL adapter
- `prisma/schema.prisma` - Complete data model with 11 entities

**Authentication System:**
- `src/lib/auth.ts` - Secure authentication with bcrypt hashing
- `src/middleware.ts` - Session verification and rate limiting

**Content Management:**
- `src/components/editor-js.tsx` - Custom Editor.js implementation
- `src/components/about-page-content.tsx` - Dynamic content rendering

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration:**
- `.env` files for database connections
- `next.config.ts` for Next.js optimization
- `tailwind.config.ts` for design system
- `tsconfig.json` for TypeScript compilation

**Security Configuration:**
- Comprehensive CSP policies
- Strict transport security
- Content validation and sanitization
- Rate limiting implementation

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies:**
- `@prisma/client` - Database ORM
- `@editorjs/editorjs` - Rich text editing
- `cloudinary` - Media optimization
- `next-auth` - Authentication
- `zustand` - State management

**Development Dependencies:**
- `@tailwindcss/postcss` - Styling pipeline
- `bun-types` - Alternative runtime support
- `eslint` - Code quality
- `typescript` - Type safety

### Integration Points

**Media Management:**
- Cloudinary integration for image optimization
- Local storage with automatic cleanup
- Duplicate detection using SHA-256 hashing

**Analytics and Monitoring:**
- Google Analytics integration
- Performance monitoring
- User behavior tracking

**Email Services:**
- Resend for transactional emails
- Contact form notifications
- Password reset functionality

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Layout Provider] --> B[Theme Provider]
    A --> C[Analytics Loader]
    B --> D[UI Components]
    C --> E[Google Analytics]
    D --> F[Editor.js]
    F --> G[Media Picker]
    G --> H[Cloudinary]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Content Management System:**
- Drag-and-drop interface for content reordering
- Rich text editing with custom tools
- Media library with duplicate detection
- Real-time preview capabilities

**Administrative Features:**
- Role-based access control
- Audit logging
- Content scheduling
- Bulk operations

**User Experience:**
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Dark/light theme switching
- Smooth animations and transitions
- Accessibility compliance

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Media Handling:**
- `src/components/media-library-browser.tsx` - Comprehensive media browser
- `src/lib/media-references.ts` - Reference tracking system
- `src/components/media-picker.tsx` - Flexible media selection

**Rich Text Editing:**
- `src/components/editor-js.tsx` - Custom Editor.js implementation
- `src/components/editor-js-image-tool.tsx` - Enhanced image handling
- `src/components/editor-js-video-tool.tsx` - Video integration

**Performance Optimization:**
- `src/components/hero-carousel.tsx` - Optimized image carousel
- `src/components/services-section.tsx` - Lazy loading components
- `src/components/news-section.tsx` - Efficient data fetching

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Authenticated: Login Success
    Loading --> Unauthenticated: Login Failed
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: Navigate
    Unauthenticated --> Login: Show Form
    Dashboard --> ContentManagement: Select Section
    ContentManagement --> Editor: Edit Content
    Editor --> Preview: Save Draft
    Preview --> Published: Publish
    Published --> Dashboard: Return
```

**Security Implementation:**
- `src/middleware.ts` - Comprehensive security middleware
- `src/lib/auth.ts` - Secure authentication system
- Rate limiting for all public endpoints

**SEO and Performance:**
- Dynamic metadata generation
- Optimized image loading
- Progressive web app features
- Core Web Vitals optimization

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [src/app/layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [src/lib/db.ts](src/lib/db.ts)
- [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts)
- [src/components/theme-provider.tsx](src/components/theme-provider.tsx)
- [tailwind.config.ts](tailwind.config.ts)
- [src/app/api/route.ts](src/app/api/route.ts)
- [src/components/about-page-content.tsx](src/components/about-page-content.tsx)
- [src/components/editor-js.tsx](src/components/editor-js.tsx)

</blog>
